## Tribus manuscript

- Current structure

 - Introduction

 - Methods

 - The structure of Tribus

 - Unsupervised learning in Tribus

 - Cell type assignment by scoring functions

 - Napari plug-in

 - Benchmarking Datasets

 - AML

 - BMMC

 - DCIS

 - HubMAP

 - [TMA (Include if the reviewers ask)]{.mark}

 - [In house dataset (Pre & Post)]{.mark}

 - Methods for comparison

 - Evaluation Metrics

 - Results

 - Overview of Tribus \[Fig 1 Highlight: interpretable, results
 > visualization\]

 - Tribus recovers fine-grained cell types as accurately as
 > human experts \[Fig 2 Detail introduction for DCIS\]

 - Tribus successfully targeted ambiguous populations
 > \[together with figure 2\]

 - The DCIS dataset has "OtherImmune" and "Unknown"
 > cell types. We successfully targeted those cell
 > types by setting undefined and other_threshold
 > when calculating the scoring function

 - Tribus outperformed other similar methods in various
 > scenarios and faster \[Fig 3\]

 - Adding levels helps with improving the accuracy \[Part of
 > Figure 3\]

 - We observed this when adjusting the logic table on the
 > AML dataset.

 - Tribus yields rapid and accurate cell type calling in large
 > whole slide imaging datasets \[Fig 4. Heatmap+UMAP+Cell
 > proportion+Nice images+functional markers\]

 - Discussion

Tribus manuscript notes discussion

- In-house dataset:

> whole slide imaging datasets \[Fig 4. Heatmap+UMAP+Cell
 > proportion+Nice images+functional markers\]

 - Discussion

Tribus manuscript notes discussion

- In-house dataset:

 - After making the table, TMA probably could be left out and used
 > only if the reviewers ask, and in that case we could even use
 > Fernando's (NKI) instead

- Table for datasets: staining method, tissue, nr of cells, nr of
 > markers, size of the dataset, original celltype calling method

- We probably do not need Fig1 panel A, the figure about where Tribus
 > is in the pipeline

- Highlight the QC → result visualization plots (heatmaps, umap,
 > barplot)

- Make it clear in pipeline figure that the hierarchical cell type
 > calling is automatic, it's done in levels but the user does not
 > have to run it and repeat it manually

- Figure2: Visual representation of match:for example correlation
 > plots and screenshots (DCIS)→ first show convincing results that
 > it works really nice, then add the accuracy metrics and running
 > times (this part could be already fig3)

- Other/Undefined: Tribus successfully groups these cells as well (do
 > not put it as a highlight, but as an addition to the DCIS plot) -
 > Tribus doesn't classify cells forcefully and it was consistent
 > with manual gating where experts could not decide on certain cell
 > phenotypes.

addition to the DCIS plot) -
 > Tribus doesn't classify cells forcefully and it was consistent
 > with manual gating where experts could not decide on certain cell
 > phenotypes.

- Supervised umap, heatmap could go to supplementary figure 2

- Adding levels is beneficial figure → part of Fig3 (barplot is main
 > results, confusion matrix might could go to the supplements)

- Pre&post data: we CAN show biology → Figure4 to describe and show
 > this dataset, show the data, UMAP, heatmaps, we do not have to go
 > deep, but show what kind of exploration we can do. Minimum to
 > show: cell proportion/sample as barplots, nice images, look into
 > the proliferating cancer cells and the fraction change should show
 > (lower in the post samples). Check he functional markers and check
 > the functional states over celltypes (uniform set of markers, so
 > try to look into how we could include things without losing too
 > much results)

Questions to discuss:

- Fig 2C UMAP → Fig 2D

- Number of cells in each cell type (DCIS): does it look good? Put it
 > in the main figure or supplementary figure?

- Benchmarking on HubMAP: should we only use donor 004?

 - Currently using donor 004, 005, 006.

 - B004 was annotated by Leiden clustering. Impure clusters were
 > split or reclustered after mapping back to the original
 > fluorescence images.

 - B005 and B006 was annotated by STELLAR (supervised method, use
 > B004 as training set.

 - **After deciding which donor to choose, add HubMAP analysis in
 > Fig 3B** (time consuming)

 > very high, we expect it to be less reliable?

## Discussion 05.12.2023

For Pre & Post dataset:

- Problem from failed stainings: such as CD4 and CD20

- It's still acceptable to only use 4 images, we only need to show
 > that it works on our in-house dataset. We don't need very
 > biological result

 > table

 - Plan B: Use different gates

 - Plan C: Use Ada's images

 > proportion will be enough) in Fig 4 (but the cohorts are from
 > collaboration labs)

ribus good (DCIS)

 > across slides... If not consider change your logic table.
